fields.name: Citra Hops
fields.description: Citra is one of the most sought-after and recognizable hop varieties in the world of craft brewing, famous for its bright and multifaceted citrus aroma. It imparts notes of grapefruit, lime, passion fruit, lychee, and melon to beer, creating a unique tropical bouquet.

specifications.Aroma Profile: Grapefruit, Lime, Passion Fruit, Lychee, Melon
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: IPA, Double IPA, Pale Ale, American Wheat

fields.name: Maris Otter Pale Malt
fields.description: Maris Otter Pale Malt is the cornerstone of British brewing heritage, a revered base malt prized by brewers worldwide for its exceptional quality and flavor. It provides a rich, slightly sweet, and biscuity malt backbone.

fields.name: SafAle US-05 Dry Ale Yeast
fields.description: The most famous and popular American ale yeast in the world. It provides a clean, crisp beer with a neutral flavor profile, allowing the hop and malt character to shine through.

fields.name: West Coast IPA - All-Grain Kit
fields.description: Brew a craft brewing classic! This kit contains all the necessary ingredients to create a bright, bitter, and incredibly aromatic IPA in the style of the US West Coast. Includes Centennial, Simcoe, and Columbus hops.

fields.name: Cascade Hops
fields.description: Classic American aroma hop with floral and citrus (grapefruit) notes. Perfect for APA and IPA.

specifications.Aroma Profile: Medium-intensity floral, citrus (grapefruit), and spicy notes.
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Porter, Barleywine, Witbier

fields.name: Caramel Malt 60L
fields.description: Caramel Malt 60L (Crystal 60L) is a versatile specialty malt that enhances beer color, flavor, and body. It imparts a copper-amber hue and notes of caramel and toffee.

specifications.Flavor Profile: Sweet, caramel, toffee, hints of toasted bread
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: Pale Ale, Amber Ale, IPA, Brown Ale, Porter, Stout, Scotch Ale

fields.name: Centennial Hops
fields.description: Known as 'Super Cascade' due to its powerful citrus profile with bright notes of lemon and grapefruit. Excellent for both bittering and intense aroma.

specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: All US Ale styles, especially IPA, Double IPA, and American Pale Ale.

fields.name: Imperial Organic Yeast A07
fields.description: Flagship liquid yeast with ~200 billion cells per pouch. Reliable, clean, and produces balanced ales with a soft mouthfeel. No starter required.

specifications.Flavor Profile: Balanced, slightly fruity, hints of citrus and stone fruit.
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: American Pale Ale, IPA, Double IPA, Porter, Stout, Amber Ale

fields.name: Mosaic Hops
fields.description: One of the most complex hops available. Imparts tropical fruit, berry, citrus, and herbal notes. Ideal for dry hopping in Hazy IPAs.

specifications.Aroma Profile: Complex and multifaceted. Tropical fruit (mango, guava), citrus (tangerine), berry (blueberry), stone fruit (peach), pine, and earthy notes.
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: IPA, Double IPA, Hazy/NEIPA, American Pale Ale, Session IPA.

fields.name: Pilsner Malt
fields.description: The lightest base malt, produced from high-quality two-row barley. Imparts a straw-like color and clean, grainy flavor. Foundation for classic lagers.

specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: Pilsner, Helles, Kolsch, Belgian Tripel, Light Lagers, Saison

fields.name: Saaz Hops
fields.description: Noble hop from the Czech Republic. Delicate, spicy, and earthy aroma. Professional choice for authentic Bohemian Pilsners.

specifications.Aroma Profile: Mild, pleasant, earthy, spicy, and floral.
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: Bohemian Pilsner, German Pilsner, Light Lagers, Belgian Ales, Lambic

fields.name: Unmalted Wheat
fields.description: Secret ingredient for Belgian Witbiers. Raw grain that provides a unique silky body and characteristic light haze. High protein for great head retention.

specifications.Protein: High (contributes to haze and head retention)
specifications.Usage: Typically 30-50% of the grist for Witbiers. Requires a cereal mash or a mash with high diastatic power malts (like Pilsner or 6-Row).
specifications.Recommended Beer Styles: Belgian Witbier, Lambic, Grand Cru, certain Saisons.
